overview: The Multiplier Grants Scheme (MGS) is implemented by the Department of Electronics and Information Technology (DeitY) to encourage collaborative R&D between industry and academic/R&D institutions for the development of commercially viable products and packages. Under the scheme, if industry supports R&D for product development at the institution level, the government provides financial support up to twice the amount contributed by industry. Proposals must be submitted jointly by industry and academic/R&D institutions.
objectives: Encourage collaborative R&D between industry and academics/R&D institutions
Develop products and packages that can be commercialized at institution level
Provide government financial support up to twice the industry contribution
Support projects in electronics and information technology
Promote capacity building in the Computer Sciences and Information Technology domain
eligibility: Startup, Incubator/Academia/Accelerator having projects in electronics & information technology
benefits: Government provides financial support up to twice the amount contributed by industry; grants are given to academic/R&D institution(s) only; supports collaborative R&D for commercializable products; funding duration preferably less than 2 years for individual industry and 3 years for industry consortium.
financial_support: The Government grants, for individual industry, would be limited to a maximum of Rs. 2.0 Crores per project and the duration of each project should, preferably, be less than 2 years; for industry consortium these figures would be Rs. 4.0 Crores and 3 years respectively. The contribution of industry and grant-in-aid from DeitY will be given to academic/R&D institution(s) only.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 2.0 Crores per project for individual industry; Rs. 4.0 Crores for industry consortium
application_process: 1. Identify a collaborative R&D project between industry and academic/R&D institution in electronics & information technology. 2. Ensure the startup/incubator/academia/accelerator has a project in electronics & IT. 3. Prepare a joint proposal from industry and institution. 4. Submit the proposal jointly by industry and academic/R&D institution. 5. Await evaluation and approval based on commercialization potential and eligibility.
implementing_agency: Department of Electronics and Information Technology (DeitY), Govt of India
caveats: The contribution of industry and grant-in-aid from DeitY will be given to academic/R&D institution(s) only
For individual industry, project duration should preferably be less than 2 years
For industry consortium, project duration should preferably be less than 3 years
Proposals must be submitted jointly by industry and academic/R&D institution
